creator: jhb@FreeBSD.org
text:
I think the patch is probably fine.  This looks to be a BIOS bug.  The failing memory BAR for the VGA device is '0xfeb00000' with a size of 256K.  However, ACPI reserves the first page of that range as a system resource (from devinfo -vr output without the patch):

nexus0
  ...
  acpi0
      ...
      I/O memory addresses:
          ...
          0xfeb00000-0xfeb00fff

FWIW, hw.pci.realloc_bars is a bit of a conservative knob that I had considered enabling by default.  I've added Warner to see what he thinks about possibly enabling this knob by default in HEAD.  The current patch is probably fine to commit as-is and is probably safe to MFC as well since it is specific to that device id.
